hero.title: Discord Reactive Images
hero.subtitle: Simple Discord voice chat integration with OBS
hero.description: Discord Reactive Images lets you display voice channel members in OBS through a single browser source. It's similar to Discord Streamkit but more flexible: upload your own images, adjust spacing and effects in real time.
hero.needDiscord: Discord must be running for full functionality
hero.gateHint: Enter the access code first, then sign in with Discord.

gate.title: Access Code
gate.description: A valid site access code is required for OBS links and image uploads.

links.title: OBS Links
links.warning: Copy one of the links and add it to OBS as a browser source. Set the width and height to match your scene. Note: image positions and sizes may change as participants join or leave — do not use this to hide sensitive information (e.g. game codes).

tutorial.step1: Welcome to Discord Reactive Images. Here you configure images for voice chat streaming. In this tutorial: your own images, OBS links, and display settings.
tutorial.step2: 'Silent' — the image shown when you're not speaking. By default, this is your dimmed Discord avatar.
tutorial.step3: To upload your own image, click the file input field and select a file from your computer.
tutorial.step4: After selecting, click 'Save' — the image will upload and update in all browser sources without reloading.
tutorial.step5: 'Reset' restores your Discord avatar instead of the uploaded file.
tutorial.step6: 'Speaking' — the image shown while you speak. By default it matches the 'silent' image but without dimming.
tutorial.step7: Uploading and saving for 'Speaking' works the same way. 'Reset' will set the same image as 'Silent'.
tutorial.step8: The 'Links' section contains URLs to add in OBS (or other software) as a browser source.
tutorial.step9: The group source shows everyone in the channel — convenient when the lineup changes often.
tutorial.step10: The individual source shows only you. Useful for making yourself larger; an active voice channel is still required.
tutorial.step11: Other participants will appear as separate individual links — you can place them on the scene in advance.
tutorial.step12: For guests, you can open the gear icon and set their own 'silent' / 'speaking' images.
tutorial.step13: In the guest settings window, upload and 'Reset' work the same way. 'Reset' restores images set by the participant themselves.
tutorial.step14: Note the warning: images may shift and resize — don't rely on them to hide sensitive information.
tutorial.step15: Here you configure the behavior and appearance of browser sources.
tutorial.step16: 'Bounce' slightly shifts the image up and down when speech starts — more noticeable, but can be distracting.
tutorial.step17: 'Show self in group view' — toggle your avatar visibility in the group source.
tutorial.step18: 'Spacing' sets the distance between avatars in group mode: positive values create gaps, negative values create overlap.
tutorial.step19: Changes are applied after clicking 'Apply' and immediately reflected in open sources.
tutorial.step20: That's all. If you need help — check the GitHub repository or the original author. Thanks for using the service!

error.notFoundHint: Check the address or return to the home page.
error.errorHint: Try refreshing the page or come back later.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.s1text: By using the Discord Reactive Images service ('Service'), you agree to these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.
terms.s2text: The Service allows you to upload images and generate links for use as Browser Sources in OBS Studio and other streaming software. Images are displayed based on voice activity in Discord.
terms.s3text: When signing in via Discord, we receive your Discord ID, username, and avatar. This data is used solely for identification and displaying your settings. We do not share it with third parties.
terms.s4text: You are responsible for the images you upload. It is prohibited to upload material that infringes copyrights, contains violence, adult content, or any other illegal content.
terms.s5text: We do not guarantee uninterrupted service operation and may discontinue or modify it without prior notice.
terms.s6text: The Service is provided 'as is'. We are not liable for any damages arising from the use or inability to use the Service.
terms.s7text: We may update these terms. The current version is always available on this page. By continuing to use the Service after changes, you accept the new terms.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1intro: When signing in via Discord, we receive:
privacy.s1li4: Email address (for identification only, not displayed publicly)
privacy.s2li1: Identifying your account in the system
privacy.s2li2: Linking your images to your Discord profile
privacy.s3text: Your data is stored in a secure database. Uploaded images are saved on the server. Data is not shared with third parties or used for advertising.
privacy.s4text: We use cookies to store your authorization session. Cookies are deleted when you log out. Authentication is not possible without cookies.
privacy.s5text: You can request deletion of all your data by contacting us. Deletion is irreversible.
privacy.s6text: Authentication is handled via Discord OAuth2. Data transmission to Discord is governed by the Discord Privacy Policy.
privacy.s7text: We may update this policy. The current version is always available on this page.
